ogTitle: Gorge Rock vs Gosnells: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Gorge Rock and Gosnells.
heading: Gorge Rock vs Gosnells — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Gorge Rock, Western Australia and Gosnells, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Gorge Rock has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Gosnells (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Gorge Rock or Gosnells safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Gorge Rock has a flood risk score of 27/100, while Gosnells scores 47/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Gorge Rock scores 47/100 for bushfire risk. Gosnells scores 45/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Gorge Rock or Gosnells?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Gorge Rock has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.88% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
